{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44516", "cvss": {"score": 7.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.6, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-532", "name": "Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File"}], "description": "### Summary

The `LoggingRestClientCustomizer` in the `web` module automatically intercepts all outgoing HTTP calls made via Spring's `RestClient` and logs the full request body, response body, and response headers. When an error response is received, this information is included in the thrown `HttpClientErrorException` message, which is logged at ERROR level by Spring's default exception handling — regardless of the application's DEBUG log level setting.

### Impact

The logged data can contain highly sensitive information including:
- Authentication credentials (JWT tokens, API keys, OAuth tokens) in request bodies or response headers
- Personal data (BSN, email addresses, case details) in request/response bodies
- Session tokens in `Set-Cookie` response headers

This data is exposed to:
- Anyone with access to application logs (stdout/log files)
- Users with access to logging aggregation tools (e.g. Grafana/Loki)
- Any Valtimo user with the admin role, through the built-in logging module (since Valtimo 12.5.0)

Leaked authentication credentials could be used to impersonate the Valtimo application against the target external API (e.g. ZGW services), compromising that API's security boundary.

Related: GHSA-hfrg-mcvw-8mch (similar sensitive data exposure in InboxHandlingService)

### Affected Code

`com.ritense.valtimo.web.logging.LoggingRestClientCustomizer#intercept` in the `web` module.

### Patched Versions

The vulnerability is fixed in:
- **12.33.0** (v12 release line) — see PR #600
- **13.26.0** (v13 release line) — see PR #599

The fix removes the request/response report, headers, and response body from the `HttpClientErrorException` constructor; only the HTTP status code and status text remain. The full request/response report is still emitted at DEBUG level (disabled in production).

### Mitigation

If you cannot upgrade to a patched version immediately, consider:
- Restricting access to application logs and the Valtimo logging module
- Adjusting the log level for `com.ritense.valtimo.web.logging` to WARN or higher (note: this only mitigates the DEBUG logging path; error responses still leak data via the exception message)", "epss": {"percentage": 0.002, "percentile": 0.09806}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-11T16:11:06Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44516"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-14T17:16:23Z", "published_at": "2026-05-11T16:11:06Z", "references": ["https://github.com/valtimo-platform/valtimo/security/advisories/GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7", "https://github.com/valtimo-platform/valtimo/pull/599", "https://github.com/valtimo-platform/valtimo/pull/600", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44516", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/valtimo-platform/valtimo/security-advisories/GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/valtimo-platform/valtimo", "summary": "Valtimo has sensitive data exposure through HTTP request/response logging in LoggingRestClientCustomizer", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:54:42Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-3jh5-rr2q-xfv7", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "12.33.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "com.ritense.valtimo:web"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 12.4.0, < 12.33.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "13.26.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "com.ritense.valtimo:web"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 13.0.0, < 13.26.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Valtimo has sensitive data exposure through HTTP request/response logging in LoggingRestClientCustomizer
